name: Do-Ahead Pumpkin Chiffon Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Dark brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the gelatin mixture in the top of a double boiler. Stir in milk, water, egg yolks, and canned pumpkin until well combined. Place over boiling water and cook, stirring constantly, until the gelatin dissolves and the mixture is heated through (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat and let cool until it mounds when dropped from a spoon. Beat egg whites stiffly and gradually add sugar. Fold in the gelatin mixture. Pour into a baked 9-inch pie shell.
For the 9-Inch Pie Shell:
No floured board needed. Roll out a 12-inch circle of pastry dough on a 14-inch square of Kaiser Quilted Foil. Lift foil and pastry into a pie plate, fitting gently onto the plate. Flute the pastry rim. Prick the bottom and sides of the pastry. Bake for 10 minutes at 450°F or until evenly brown; the foil prevents over-browning. Cool. Spoon filling into the shell. Wrap loosely in foil. Refrigerate overnight. Serves 8. Garnish with whipped cream if desired.
